meta.notes: v1.4: Calendar refilled after 2026-06-26 exhaustion. 8-week arc starting 2026-07-07. Theme arc: Week 1 Community Integration (the four commitments), Weeks 2-4 Objection Handling (foreigner objection head-on, then timeline/Foundation phase, then worldschooling/EU/entity practicalities), Weeks 5-7 Three Pillars deep dive (echo of v1.3 arc with fresh angles), Week 8 EOI Mechanics + Investor Case close. Cadence: IG Mon/Wed/Fri, FB Wed/Fri, LinkedIn Tue/Thu. 43 total slots.

weekly_cadence.notes: LinkedIn avoids Mon/Wed/Fri to dodge Flow OS collision. Crete cadence approx 7 posts/week across all three platforms.

content_slots.brief: Introduce the four commitments framing directly. Local people benefit — not just local property values. Name the four: Employment (local hiring across construction, agriculture, operations), Supply (sourcing from Cretan producers and tradespeople), Housing (fair-rate residential where capacity allows), Culture (restoration that honours heritage rather than erases it). Position as deliberate operating choices, not marketing language. LinkedIn investor-professional voice.

Visual-led post about the villages we work in. Traditional Cretan stone, existing character, existing residents. Short caption anchoring the "preserve, don't replace" position. Foundation-phase honest — the work hasn't happened yet but the intent is specific and deliberate.

Employment commitment in depth. Why local hiring across all project phases is not tokenism but the only way this model works. Regenerative agriculture and biodynamic farming need people who understand the land generationally, not imported labour. Village restoration needs Cretan stone masons and craftspeople. Wellness centre operations need local staff to remain community-embedded. Professional tone, no fluff.

One-line truth about local sourcing. Something anchored to the Supply commitment — Cretan producers and tradespeople as the default, not the exception. Anti-cliché, no "farm to table" phrasing. Grounded.

Long-form Facebook post laying out all four commitments with more room to breathe. Employment, Supply, Housing (fair-rate residential where capacity allows), Culture. Honest that these are commitments the project has made publicly at creteprojects.com and will be measured against. Foundation-phase honesty about being pre-acquisition.

Name the objection directly. "Yes, we are foreigners." Address the "just another foreigner coming in to take over Greece" concern head-on rather than dance around it. Do not overclaim Greek connection the founders do not have (no family ties, no prior residency, first project of this scale). Instead pivot to what the founders will and will not do. Anti-cliché, direct, professional. Foundation-phase honesty applied to identity.
One-line truth about the difference between arriving and integrating. Anti-gentrification framing without saying "gentrification". Punchy.

Visual-led post about the pattern of showing up somewhere without alienating. Reference the founders' existing Cretan vineyard as evidence of operating pattern (not as a Crete Projects credential — distinct property, distinct scope, same operating principle). Active relationships with past owners and neighbours. Concrete, not abstract.

Long-form Facebook post on the corporate structure honesty. Cyprus holding company in completion. Greek operating entity to follow. First-stage investment gated on Cyprus completion — no investor capital moves until the vehicle to hold it is real. Frame as evidence of committing to build inside the Greek system rather than around it. Foundation-phase transparency about what is done versus in progress.

The anti-model post. What Crete Projects deliberately is NOT building. No gated compounds, no all-inclusive resort model, no gentrification playbook, no soul-stripping restoration. Position these as active choices with trade-offs rather than marketing negatives. Direct, honest about what it costs to make each of these choices.

Address "how do we know you'll actually do what you say?" objection directly. Foundation-phase honesty about where the project is now, what is committed at creteprojects.com, what will be measurable and by when. Honest about the risk investors take at Foundation phase versus later.

Investor-angle response to timeline objections. Why the harder, slower sequencing (Cyprus structure first, then acquisition, then ground-breaking, then community engagement with mayor and landowners) is the right sequence rather than the fast sequence. Explain the trade-off honestly. Speak to sophisticated investors who have seen fast-and-loose developments fail.
Single-line truth about slow-building versus fast-building. Anti-cliché — no "good things take time" fluff. Something specific to the project's actual choice.

Long-form Facebook post walking through the actual sequencing publicly. Acquisition begins → community engagement with mayor and landowners → ground-breaking → construction phased across pillars. Honest that step 2 has not happened yet and is deliberately gated on step 1. Foundation-phase transparency.

The credibility question head-on. First project of this scale for the founders. What that means for risk. What compensates for the lack of track record on this specific model — the deliberate choices, the community commitments, the operating pattern already demonstrable at the vineyard. Foundation-phase honesty applied to founder credentials.

The worldschooling logistics angle. What families ask most: schooling continuity, healthcare access, safety, community fit. Address at a high level without pretending the answers are all worked out. Foundation-phase honesty about what is under active planning versus what is not yet resolved.

The EU residency and entity practicalities question. Common investor questions about Greek property ownership as a foreigner, EU visa pathways, Cyprus versus Greek entity structure for international investors. Speak to what is being set up and why. Do not overclaim on legal or tax specifics that have not been finalised. Direct on the uncertainty where it exists.
Single-line truth about what makes a place work for a family versus just a couple. Anti-cliché, grounded.

Long-form Facebook post on the family-in-Crete practicalities. Structure of a stay. What day-to-day looks like for parents working remotely and children learning inside a worldschooling model. Concrete rather than aspirational.

Follow-up on entity structure specifics that are appropriate to share publicly. Cyprus holding company in completion. Greek operating entity to follow. Why this structure rather than a single-jurisdiction option. Speak to the sophistication of the intended investor audience.

The agricultural land pillar reintroduced. What regenerative agroforestry actually means in the Crete context. Layered planting, soil health, water cycle, long-term yield over short-term extraction. Visual-led.

The investment case for agricultural land in Crete reintroduced with fresh angle. Focus this time on the intersection of agroforestry as an asset class and the EU framework for regenerative agriculture funding. Treat as alternative asset with specific structural characteristics, not as farm.
Single-line truth about the relationship between soil and generational wealth. Punchy, anti-cliché.
Biodynamic farming reintroduced for a wider audience. What it is, what it isn't (not astrology, not spirituality), why we've chosen it over conventional organic. Practical, educational.
Agro-tourism as a revenue stream within the agricultural land pillar. Workshops, harvest weeks, structured visits. Position as complementary to village accommodation stays rather than competitive.
The village restoration pillar reintroduced. Traditional Cretan stone architecture as the material starting point. Visual-led, atmospheric.

The economics of stone restoration versus new-build, reintroduced with fresh angle. Focus this time on why the harder path also compounds better over time — heritage buildings gain value in a way new-build does not, particularly in EU-protected zones. Speak as a builder, not a marketer.
Single-line truth about the difference between preserving and renovating. Anti-cliché — no "old world charm" language.
Worldschooling families as the specific target audience for village accommodation, reintroduced. Why 2 to 8 week stays specifically. The thinking behind the length.

Premium accommodation category reintroduced. Pricing logic and audience fit. Fresh angle this time: why the premium segment is small, specific, and structurally under-served in Crete compared to Santorini or Mykonos.

The wellness centre vision reintroduced. Gym, cold plunge, sauna, physiotherapy, yoga and pilates space, breathwork sessions, visiting practitioners. Honest about phasing — the wellness build will be phased and not day-one complete.

Wellness as infrastructure, not amenity — reintroduced with fresh angle. Focus this time on why treating it as infrastructure changes the operating decisions (staffing, hours, equipment lifecycle, integration with village stays). Speak to operators.
Single-line truth about the difference between wellness as backdrop and wellness as practice. Honest, anti-cliché.
Visiting practitioners model reintroduced. Who rotates through, at what cadence, in what disciplines. Practical rather than aspirational.

The performance case for environments like this, reintroduced. Why entrepreneurs and high-performers route deliberately through places that support recovery. Fresh angle: connect this to why the project targets location-independent families specifically rather than pure retreat guests.

The shape of the early audience — reintroduced with fresh framing. Who has registered interest so far, in qualitative terms. Anonymised, no specific numbers, no partner names. The pattern of the people showing up.

How EOI works mechanically — reintroduced. Timeline from EOI through to Cyprus completion, entity setup, first-stage investment window. Transparent about the Foundation phase gating and why EOI precedes anything else.
The asset thesis reintroduced in one visual plus one caption. Land plus restoration plus wellness as combined value. Not three separate opportunities — one integrated thesis.

The two doors into Crete Projects reintroduced. Member experience door versus investor opportunity door. Same project, two entry paths, different commitment shapes. Honest about which door most people should walk through.

Entity structure explained in appropriate detail given Cyprus completion status. Do not speculate on tax specifics that have not been finalised. Focus on what is confirmed: Cyprus holding, Greek operating entity, first-stage investment gated on Cyprus completion.
Single-line CTA-led post. Register interest at creteprojects.com. No urgency tricks, just clarity about why now is the right window to be on the list.
Closing the loop. What early registration gives you at Foundation phase that later registration will not. Honest, not pressure-based.

Closing post of the arc. What being on the list actually means in practice at this stage. Not fluff — specific about what happens next in the sequence and what invitees can expect from the founders in terms of transparency.
